title: Doryopteris magdalenensis
taxonomy: Doryopteris magdalenensis belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and the order Polypodiales. It is a species of fern, specifically a type of tree fern. The genus Doryopteris is a group of tropical and subtropical ferns. Doryopteris magdalenensis is native to South America, where it grows in humid and tropical environments. This species is characterized by its distinctive fronds and robust habit.

intro: Doryopteris magdalenensis, also known as the Magdalena tree fern, is a species of plant in the family Pteridaceae, characterized by its large, leathery fronds and creeping rhizomes. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Doryopteris magdalenensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Doryopteris magdalenensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and ointments to treat various health conditions.
